formulas.name: Objective Function
formulas.variables: Z - Objective function value, c - Coefficients, x - Decision variables
formulas.notes: Represents the objective function in a linear programming problem

formulas.name: Constraints
formulas.formula: ax1 + ax2 + ... + axn ≤ b (≤ constraint), ax1 + ax2 + ... + axn = b (equal constraint), ax1 + ax2 + ... + axn ≥ b (≥ constraint)
formulas.variables: a - Coefficients, x - Decision variables, b - Right-hand side value
formulas.notes: Represents the constraints in a linear programming problem

formulas.name: Feasible Region
formulas.formula: The set of all points (x1, x2, ..., xn) that satisfy all constraints
formulas.notes: The region where the optimal solution lies

formulas.name: Optimal Solution
formulas.formula: The point in the feasible region that maximizes or minimizes the objective function
formulas.notes: The solution that satisfies all constraints and maximizes or minimizes the objective function

formulas.name: Corner Point Method
formulas.formula: Find all corner points of the feasible region and evaluate the objective function at each point to find the optimal solution
formulas.notes: A method to find the optimal solution by evaluating the objective function at all corner points of the feasible region

formulas.name: Simplex Method
formulas.formula: An iterative method to find the optimal solution by moving from one basic feasible solution to another
formulas.notes: An algorithm to find the optimal solution by systematically moving from one basic feasible solution to another

formulas.name: Dual Problem
formulas.formula: Find the dual problem by interchanging the rows and columns of the original problem
formulas.variables: x - Decision variables, y - Dual variables
formulas.notes: A problem that is derived from the original problem by interchanging the rows and columns

formulas.name: Weak Duality Theorem
formulas.formula: If x is a feasible solution to the primal problem and y is a feasible solution to the dual problem, then cx ≤ by
formulas.variables: x - Decision variables, y - Dual variables
formulas.notes: A theorem that states that the optimal value of the primal problem is less than or equal to the optimal value of the dual problem

formulas.name: Strong Duality Theorem
formulas.formula: If x is an optimal solution to the primal problem and y is an optimal solution to the dual problem, then cx = by
formulas.variables: x - Decision variables, y - Dual variables
formulas.notes: A theorem that states that the optimal value of the primal problem is equal to the optimal value of the dual problem
